root_text.sn48.71-82:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gaṅgā nadī pācīnaninnā pācīnapoṇā pācīnapabbhārā;
root_text.sn48.71-82:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcindriyāni bhāvento pañcindriyāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro.
root_text.sn48.71-82:1.3: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcindriyāni bhāvento pañcindriyāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro?
root_text.sn48.71-82:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddhindriyaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ,
root_text.sn48.71-82:1.8: paññindriyaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn48.71-82:1.9: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcindriyāni bhāvento pañcindriyāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro”ti.

translation_text.sn48.71-82:1.1: “Mendicants, the Ganges river slants, slopes, and inclines to the east.
translation_text.sn48.71-82:1.2: In the same way, a mendicant developing and cultivating the five faculties slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.
translation_text.sn48.71-82:1.4: It’s when a mendicant develops the faculties of faith,
translation_text.sn48.71-82:1.8: and wisdom, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn48.71-82:1.9: That’s how a mendicant developing and cultivating the five faculties slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.”
translation_text.sn48.71-82:2.1: Six on slanting to the east,
translation_text.sn48.71-82:2.2: and six on slanting to the ocean;
translation_text.sn48.71-82:2.4: and that’s how this chapter is recited.
